title: Deputy / Technical Superintendent
description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

  1. Achieve high standards of safe and pollution-free operations of vessels by ensuring that company policies related to Health, Safety, Security, Environment and Quality Assurance (HSEQA) are effectively implemented
  2. You will be responsible for overall Management of assigned LNG and other LPG vessels. This will include close communication with all stakeholders, compliance to owners and charterers requirements, close monitoring of Marine and Technical Operations, Daily maintenance and repairs, Budgeting and control, Surveys, certification, Inspections and Docking.
  3. Keep abreast of international regulations / legal requirements and ensure assigned vessels comply to these.
  4. Provide Technical assistance and advice to ships.
  5. Carry out periodical technical and operational compliance audit and ensure vessel is always operational ready.
  6. Assist in investigations of all insurance related matter.
  7. Liaise closely with Procurement team for sourcing technical and operational supplies required at most economical and competitive rates.
  8. Propose annual operating budget for assigned vessels by taking view of forthcoming repairs maintenance, statutory,
  9. Flag and Port Requirements and maintain vessels within budgets.
  10. Closely monitor and review vessel's conditions to ensure that she complies with Port State Control and Oil Majors regulations at all times
  11. Effectively and efficiently carry out routine vessel inspections, dry-docking and major repairs
  12. Plan and implement corrective actions for defects that may exist in the vessels and ensure compliance with planned maintenance schedule for assigned vessel
  13. Monitor and ensure that all applicable vessel certifications are valid, and surveys / renewals are planned well in time
  14. Ensure fleet vessels performance is within charter party requirements and Company goals.
  15. Undertake technical related assignments and projects as and when required
  16. This position required frequent travel overseas and estimated up to 120 days or more travel to attend vessels in various ports worldwide.

JOB REQUIREMENTS

  1. Educational Qualification should have COC Class 1 for G.G Vessels (LNG or LPG Vessels)
  2. Must have sailed on LNG or LPG carrier’s in the Rank of Chief Engineer.
  3. Your hands-on experience in both the construction and maintenance of Cargo Tanks (Membrane and Mossberg type) and propulsion systems (steam and WinGD) is a testament to your competence and skill in this field.
  4. Proficient in PMS, ISM, PSC, Flag & Class requirements.
  5. We value continuity and stability and, therefore, prefer candidates who have remained with the same company for the last five years. Experience in a Japanese Shipyard for a New Building would be preferred.
  6. Previous working experience of at least two years handling Foreign Going vessels as Technical Superintendent.

postedCompany.description:
Executive Ship Management (ESM) is a premier management company valued by its clients and partners in the industry for its high end professional expertise and services. It is headquartered in Singapore with offices in 12 cities across the world.


As a trusted and dependable manager, ESM offers a holistic package of ship management services that includes a keen eye on the budget and cost optimization through intervention of technology for its Ship owners. ESM commits that the crew sailing the company-managed ships is recruited and appropriately trained in-house. Each crew member, be it an officer or a rating is put through a rigorous ship-specific training regime at its training centre, Samundra Institute of Maritime Studies (SIMS). Over 80% of the ESM-managed ship’s officers are from cadets trained in-house at SIMS and the Company is envisioned to be uniquely positioned to run its entire managed fleet by officers trained since cadetship.


With an equally formidable and expert shore-based technical and marine team, ESM is well regarded in the industry for its expertise, be it tankers (chemical, crude and product tankers), dry ships (bulk carriers, containers) as well as a wide range of gas carriers. We ensure all vessels including the tankers under our management meet the commercial, technical and crewing requirements and efficiently run at all times to the satisfaction of the owners and charterers.


Executive Ship Management is one of the core establishments and genesis for the founders of the Executive Group of Companies which comprises of six other Marine Services related Businesses.
postedCompany.name: EXECUTIVE SHIP MANAGEMENT PTE LTD
